title: RetrievalResponse
description: Standard response returned by any retrieval module to the backend adapter. All three modules (vector, keyword, CLaRa) return this exact shape. Derived from vector_retrieval/src/retrieval/retriever.py. The 'score' field meaning differs per method — see notes.

query.description: The original query string echoed back.
query.example: What are the main risks of RAG hallucination?

method.description: The retrieval method that produced this response.

results.description: Top-k retrieved chunks ordered by score descending (rank 1 = best match).

rank.description: 1-based rank position. Rank 1 is the most relevant result.

source.description: Original filename of the source document.

text.description: Full text content of the retrieved chunk.

score.description: Relevance score. Range and meaning differ by method: vector = L2-derived similarity in (0, 1]; keyword = BM25 score (unbounded, higher is better); clara = method-specific confidence score. All are normalised so higher = more relevant.

citation.description: Formatted citation string for display and LLM prompt injection. Format: '[<document_title> | <chunk_id>]'.

metadata.description: File-level metadata carried through from ingestion.

example.query: What are the main risks of RAG hallucination?
